name: MikroTik hAP ax3 US version
title: MikroTik hAP ax3 US version
title_extended: MikroTik hAP ax3 US version - Wi-Fi 6, Powerful 1.8 GHz ARM CPU, 802.11AX + WAVE2, Strong Dual-Band Dual-Chain Wireless (up to 5.5 DBI), 4 Gigabit Ethernet ports, PoE-In & PoE-Out, WPA3, 1 GB RAM + 128 MB NAND, USB 3.0 port, US version
short_description: Wi-Fi 6, Powerful 1.8 GHz ARM CPU, 802.11AX + WAVE2, Strong Dual-Band Dual-Chain Wireless (up to 5.5 DBI), 4 Gigabit Ethernet ports, PoE-In & PoE-Out, WPA3, 1 GB RAM + 128 MB NAND, USB 3.0 port, US version
description: MikroTik's hAP ax router series is a collection of home Wi-Fi routers introduced in 2023, featuring Wi-Fi 6 technology support. Designed for small homes, apartments, and small offices, the series offers a range of models to cater to various needs. The devices come equipped with powerful wireless chips, providing increased speed and stronger signal in both 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz spectrums. They boast modern CPUs, ample memory, and multiple Ethernet ports, ensuring smooth and reliable connectivity for various tasks. With the addition of advanced encryption support, the series prioritizes security. Overall, the hAP router series delivers high performance, scalability, and affordability for home networking needs.

detailed_description.heading: Empowering Wi-Fi 6 Technology
detailed_description.text: MikroTik's hAP ax router series is a collection of home Wi-Fi routers introduced in 2023, featuring Wi-Fi 6 technology support. Designed for small homes, apartments, and small offices, the series offers a range of models to cater to various needs. The devices come equipped with powerful wireless chips, providing increased speed and stronger signal in both 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz spectrums. They boast modern CPUs, ample memory, and multiple Ethernet ports, ensuring smooth and reliable connectivity for various tasks. With the addition of advanced encryption support, the series prioritizes security. Overall, the hAP router series delivers high performance, scalability, and affordability for home networking needs.

detailed_description.heading: Unmatched Power and Coverage
detailed_description.text: Welcome to the world of cutting-edge home networking with the hAP ax³, MikroTik’s most powerful AX device with unparalleled wireless network coverage. Equipped with a modern quad-core ARM CPU running at 1.8 GHz and boasting 1GB of RAM and 128 MB NAND, this powerhouse router handles even the most demanding tasks with ease. From complex firewall rules to IPsec hardware encryption and advanced routing, hAP ax³ ensures your family's browsing, streaming, gaming, and more proceed without interruption.

detailed_description.heading: AX Wireless Technology
detailed_description.text: Experience up to 40% higher speed in the 5 GHz spectrum and 90% higher speed in the 2.4 GHz spectrum, revolutionizing your home network speeds. Thanks to potent external antennas reaching gain up to 5.5 dBi, Wi-Fi Boosters and other tricks become a thing of the past. Enjoy smooth and fast connectivity throughout your entire apartment, leaving behind lag and dropped connections. The hAP ax³ has two RP-SMA Female connectors, which means it is possible to connect an external antenna. It comes equipped with 4x Gigabit Ethernet ports and a 2.5 Gigabit port, ensuring seamless wired connections as well.

detailed_description.heading: Enhanced Safety
detailed_description.text: MikroTik prioritizes your safety and peace of mind. In collaboration with EU lawmakers, hAP ax³ fully complies with all necessary safety policies. Rest assured with a range of power choices, such as DC Jack & PoE-in, to minimize concerns about possible downtimes. Embrace the enhanced safety brought by the latest RouterOS v7 updates and the incorporation of WPA3 advanced encryption support, providing unparalleled software security for your network.

detailed_description.heading: Access Point Excellence
detailed_description.text: Summing it up, the hAP ax³ stands among the fastest, safest, and most powerful choices in the home access point world, whether through wired or wireless connections. Its advanced networking software - RouterOS - sets new standards and offers limitless possibilities, without paywalls or confusing subscriptions.

detailed_description.heading: Unlocking New Possibilities
detailed_description.text: As the demand for higher wireless speeds grows, the hAP ax³ breaks barriers and opens up countless new use cases. Say goodbye to waiting for large media files to download and upload; the hAP ax³ empowers you to work seamlessly with large files over the wireless network, saving you precious time.

comments.name: Sandy
comments.text:
I have several mikrotik devices already so when my living room needed a new router it was an easy choice also I have already bought one for my son for his new house. 
I work in networks and I like the power and options and the constant firmware updates. While I don't use 100th of what these devices do I have always had to move on when reaching a limit of what other devices can't do. With Mikrotik I have never reached that point.

comments.name: Joao
comments.text: Definitely the best router I’ve ever owned! It offers a wealth of features and capabilities, though it does require some technical knowledge to make the most of it. The learning curve can be steep at first, but the value it delivers for the price is outstanding. I’m very satisfied!

comments.name: Seán
comments.text:
I bought this router to run Syncthing docker due to its ARM x64 CPU and 1GB RAM.  This turned out to be tricky to properly configure, but has the advantage in that I can sync large folders between my phone, laptop, etc. without a subscription or the energy cost of running a PC / server.  

One thing that took me by surprise is its Wi-Fi performance.  It completely covers our house on the 5GHz band alone, hitting over 50Mbps on speed tests in the weakest spots in the outer rooms that I could only achieve with our Tenda mesh kit up until now.  Even with the mesh nodes, my phone usually drops to the 2.4GHz more than a room apart from a mesh node. If the Wi-Fi remains stable over the coming weeks, I plan using this as our main Wi-Fi network and doing away with the mesh.  No other single router has been able to reliably cover our whole house with Wi-Fi until now on the 2.4GHz band and certainly not the 5GHz band.  With OpenSpeedTest over Wi-Fi 6 standing near the router, it has no problem maxing out the Gigabit Ethernet port on my PC.  On the 2.4GHz band it gets about 300Mbps up and down and drops to around 80Mbps in the weakest areas of the house.  

I also tested this router with the MikroTik LHG LTE18, which it can power over its PoE WAN port, so only requires a single power socket to power both.

comments.name: Jānis
comments.text: Works very good. I have a 1 Gbps internet connection and, as expected, wired connection gives me full speed. I reached more than 800Mbps using WiFi 6 in the same room and more than 500Mps while being in a different room which is much more than I expected. And the WiFi connection is stable even in different rooms. You may want to adjust the angle of antennas to get a better signal. Very simple initial configuration and very powerful configuration options for advanced users. I highly recommend this WiFi router.

comments.name: gerard
comments.text: A++ on the hardware so far. C- for the software. ROS v7.5 introduced a certificate chain import bug. The only workaround (downgrading ROS to <7.5, import certificates, and upgrade ROS) is no longer available (device only runs v7.5+). This prevents me from using the router until the issue is fixed.

comments.name: Anton
comments.text:
As a wired router this product is outstanding, as all wired mikrotik products are.
However, the WiFi on this is a complete train-wreck.
A single brick walls drops the WiFi signal from -50 dbi to -70 and speeds drop from 500 mbps to less then 100. I have no idea what kind of antennas this abomination has, but the WiFi coverage is pretty much the same as hAP AC Light I had before. Zero improvements for signal strength or connection stability observed.
If you are looking for the good WiFi connectivity - looks elsewhere. If you need a good wired router with okay'ish WiFi - go ahead.
